description: PFC command documentation index for quick lookup and LLM-assisted command discovery

ball.description: Commands for creating, modifying, and managing ball objects in PFC discrete element simulations

commands.name: accumulate-stress
commands.short_description: Enable/disable stress accumulation during cycling (reduces performance by ~15%)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball accumulate-stress <bool>')

commands.name: attribute
commands.short_description: Set ball attributes (density, displacement, force, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: Ball.set_* methods (e.g., Ball.set_density())

commands.name: clump
commands.short_description: Create clump from balls or add balls to existing clump as pebbles
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball clump ...') - Python SDK cannot convert balls to clump pebbles

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: Create a single ball with specified attributes
commands.python_alternative: itasca.ball.create(radius, centroid, id=None)

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete balls based on range specification
commands.python_alternative: Ball.delete() - requires iteration

commands.name: distribute
commands.short_description: Distribute balls with overlaps until target porosity achieved
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball distribute ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: export
commands.short_description: Export balls to geometry sets for post-processing or STL export
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball export geometry ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: extra
commands.short_description: Set ball extra variables (any type: number, vector, string)
commands.python_alternative: Ball.set_extra(slot, value) - requires iteration

commands.name: fix
commands.short_description: Fix specified ball velocities (constant motion)
commands.python_alternative: Ball.set_fix(component, True) - requires iteration

commands.name: free
commands.short_description: Free specified ball velocities (motion reflects forces)
commands.python_alternative: Ball.set_fix(component, False) - requires iteration

commands.name: generate
commands.short_description: Generate balls with various packing patterns (cubic, hexagonal, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.ball.create() - individual ball creation only

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Assign balls to groups
commands.python_alternative: Ball.set_group()

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Track ball properties over time
commands.python_alternative: Manual tracking with Python callbacks

commands.name: initialize
commands.short_description: Modify ball attributes (synonym for ball attribute)
commands.python_alternative: Same as ball attribute: Ball.set_* methods - requires iteration

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List ball information (ID, radius, contacts, fixity, position, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: Ball getter methods - requires iteration

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Set ball surface properties
commands.python_alternative: Ball.set_prop()

commands.name: results
commands.short_description: Configure ball results logic for efficient post-processing snapshots
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball results ...') - Python SDK does not provide results configuration

commands.name: tolerance
commands.short_description: Set contact detection tolerances (constant, extent, velocity)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball tolerance ...') - Python SDK does not provide tolerance settings

commands.name: trace
commands.short_description: Add particle trace to record and visualize ball motion path
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball trace ...') - Python SDK does not provide trace creation

commands.name: tractions
commands.short_description: Compute contact tractions using Voronoi tessellation for stress initialization
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('ball tractions ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

notes: Ball commands typically operate on ranges (groups, spatial regions, or all balls)
Use Python SDK for fine-grained control and complex logic
Use commands for batch operations and initial model setup
Ball generation with packing patterns requires commands (not available in Python SDK)

wall.description: Commands for creating, modifying, and managing wall objects (faceted boundaries) in PFC

commands.name: active-sides
commands.short_description: Specify which side(s) of facets are active (both, top, bottom, neither)
commands.python_alternative: Facet.set_active(code) - requires iteration over facets

commands.name: addfacet
commands.short_description: Add a facet to an existing wall
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall addfacet ...') - Python SDK does not provide facet creation

commands.name: attribute
commands.short_description: Set wall attributes (velocity, displacement, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: Wall.set_* methods (e.g., Wall.set_vel())

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: Create walls by specifying vertices directly
commands.python_alternative: Complex manual vertex creation using Python SDK

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete walls based on range specification
commands.python_alternative: Wall.delete() - requires iteration

commands.name: export
commands.short_description: Export walls to geometry sets for post-processing or STL export
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall export geometry ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: extra
commands.short_description: Set wall or facet extra variables (any type: string, int, float, vector)
commands.python_alternative: Wall.set_extra() or Facet.set_extra() - requires iteration

commands.name: generate
commands.short_description: Generate walls with standard geometries (box, cylinder, cone, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: Manual vertex calculation and wall creation

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Assign walls to groups
commands.python_alternative: Wall.set_group()

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Track wall properties over time (displacement, velocity, force, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall history ...') - Python SDK does not provide history recording

commands.name: import
commands.short_description: Import walls from STL/DXF files or geometry sets
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall import ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: initialize
commands.short_description: Modify wall attributes (synonym for wall attribute)
commands.python_alternative: Same as wall attribute: Wall.set_* methods

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List wall information (ID, name, facets, contacts, position, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: Wall getter methods - requires iteration

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Set wall surface properties
commands.python_alternative: Wall.set_prop()

commands.name: resolution
commands.short_description: Modify contact-resolution strategy (full, reduced, none)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall resolution ...') - global contact resolution strategy

commands.name: results
commands.short_description: Configure wall results logic for efficient post-processing snapshots
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall results ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: rotate
commands.short_description: Rotate walls about specified axis and point
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall rotate ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: servo
commands.short_description: Control wall velocity using servo-mechanism to maintain target force
commands.python_alternative: Wall.servo_set_* methods - requires iteration

commands.name: tolerance
commands.short_description: Set contact detection tolerances (automatic, constant, extent, velocity)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('wall tolerance ...') - low-level algorithmic control

commands.name: velocity-conveyor
commands.short_description: Assign rotational conveyor velocity to simulate rotating wall
commands.python_alternative: Facet.set_conveyor() - requires manual calculation and iteration

notes: Walls are composed of triangular facets (Facet objects)
Wall generation commands (box, cylinder) are not available in Python SDK
Use Python SDK for programmatic wall manipulation
Walls can be moved via velocity or displacement attributes

clump.description: Clump object creation, modification, and template management commands. Clumps are rigid aggregates of overlapping or touching balls (pebbles) that behave as single rigid bodies.

commands.name: accumulate-stress
commands.short_description: Enable/disable stress accumulation during cycling (reduces performance)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump accumulate-stress <bool>')

commands.name: attribute
commands.short_description: Set clump attributes (density, velocity, force, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: Clump.set_* methods

commands.name: break
commands.short_description: Break clumps into pebbles by ID or plane
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump break ...') - Python SDK cannot break clumps

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: Create a single clump with specified pebbles and attributes
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump create ...') - Python SDK cannot directly create clumps with pebbles

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete clumps based on range specification
commands.python_alternative: Clump.delete() - requires iteration

commands.name: distribute
commands.short_description: Distribute clumps to achieve target porosity (allows overlaps)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump distribute ...') - Python SDK does not support clump distribution

commands.name: export
commands.short_description: Export clumps to geometry sets for post-processing or STL export
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump export geometry ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: extra
commands.short_description: Set clump extra variables (any type: number, vector, string)
commands.python_alternative: Clump.set_extra(slot, value) - requires iteration

commands.name: fix
commands.short_description: Fix specified clump velocities (constant motion)
commands.python_alternative: Clump.set_fix(component, True) - requires iteration

commands.name: free
commands.short_description: Free specified clump velocities (motion reflects forces)
commands.python_alternative: Clump.set_fix(component, False) - requires iteration

commands.name: generate
commands.short_description: Generate multiple non-overlapping clumps from templates
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump generate ...') - Python SDK cannot directly generate clumps with templating system

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Assign clumps to groups
commands.python_alternative: Clump.set_group()

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Track clump properties over time
commands.python_alternative: Manual tracking with Python callbacks

commands.name: initialize
commands.short_description: Modify clump attributes (synonym for clump attribute)
commands.python_alternative: Same as clump attribute: Clump.set_* methods

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List clump information (ID, pebbles, contacts, position, etc.)
commands.python_alternative: Clump getter methods - requires iteration

commands.name: order
commands.short_description: Set rotational EOM order (2 or 4, 3D only)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump order ...') - Python SDK does not provide order setting

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Set clump surface properties
commands.python_alternative: Clump.set_prop()

commands.name: replicate
commands.short_description: Create a single clump instance from a template
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump replicate ...') - Python SDK cannot directly replicate clumps from templates

commands.name: results
commands.short_description: Configure clump results logic for efficient post-processing snapshots
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump results ...') - Python SDK does not provide results configuration

commands.name: rotate
commands.short_description: Rotate clumps about specified axis and point
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump rotate ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: scale
commands.short_description: Scale clumps by diameter, volume, or FISH function
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump scale ...') - no Python SDK equivalent

commands.name: template
commands.short_description: Create, delete, import, or export clump templates
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump template ...') - Python SDK does not provide direct clump template management

commands.name: tolerance
commands.short_description: Set contact detection tolerances (constant, extent, velocity)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump tolerance ...') - Python SDK does not provide tolerance settings

commands.name: trace
commands.short_description: Add clump trace to record and visualize clump motion path
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump trace ...') - Python SDK does not provide trace creation

notes: Clumps are rigid bodies composed of pebbles (component balls)
Clump templates enable efficient replication of complex shapes
clump generate creates non-overlapping clumps; clump distribute allows overlaps for target porosity
Most clump operations (template, generate, distribute) not available in Python SDK

contact.description: Commands for configuring contact models and managing contact properties between objects

commands.name: activate
commands.short_description: Control contact activation state
commands.python_alternative: Contact.activate(flag) - requires iteration

commands.name: apply-group
commands.short_description: Apply contact group names to contacting pieces or bodies
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact apply-group ...') - requires manual iteration

commands.name: cmat add
commands.short_description: Add new entry to CMAT (Contact Model Assignment Table)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact cmat add ...') - no Python SDK CMAT API

commands.name: cmat apply
commands.short_description: Apply CMAT to existing contacts (overwrites contact models)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact cmat apply ...') - no Python SDK CMAT API

commands.name: cmat default
commands.short_description: Set default slot(s) of CMAT
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact cmat default ...') - no Python SDK CMAT API

commands.name: cmat list
commands.short_description: List CMAT entries with their indices, models, and properties
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact cmat list') - no Python SDK CMAT API

commands.name: cmat modify
commands.short_description: Modify existing CMAT entry at specified index
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact cmat modify ...') - no Python SDK CMAT API

commands.name: cmat proximity
commands.short_description: Set contact detection distance in CMAT entry
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact cmat proximity ...') - no Python SDK CMAT API

commands.name: cmat remove
commands.short_description: Remove entry from CMAT at specified index
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact cmat remove ...') - no Python SDK CMAT API

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete contacts in specified range
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact delete range ...') - no direct SDK method

commands.name: detection
commands.short_description: Control automatic contact detection on/off
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact detection ...') - no direct SDK method

commands.name: fix
commands.short_description: Fix contact forces at current values (freeze forces)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('contact fix ...') - no Python SDK fix method

commands.name: group
commands.short_description: Assign contacts to groups
commands.python_alternative: Contact.set_group(), group(), group_remove(), groups(), in_group()

commands.name: inhibit
commands.short_description: Inhibit (disable) contacts temporarily
commands.python_alternative: Contact.set_inhibit(flag), inhibit()

commands.name: method
commands.short_description: Execute contact model methods
commands.python_alternative: Contact.method()

commands.name: model
commands.short_description: Assign contact model to existing contacts
commands.python_alternative: Contact.set_model(model_name) - requires iteration

commands.name: persist
commands.short_description: Set contact persistence flag (prevent automatic deletion)
commands.python_alternative: Contact.set_persist(flag), Contact.persist()

commands.name: property
commands.short_description: Set contact model properties for existing contacts
commands.python_alternative: Contact.set_prop(property_name, value) - requires iteration

notes: Contact models define mechanical behavior at contact points
Common contact models: linear, linearpbond, linearcbond, hertz
Contact properties are model-specific (e.g., 'kn' for normal stiffness)
Use 'contact model' commands to assign and configure models

model.description: System-level commands for controlling simulation execution, configuration, and state management

commands.name: calm
commands.short_description: Set all linear and rotational velocities to zero (remove kinetic energy)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model calm') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: clean
commands.short_description: Create contacts, initialize properties, update spatial structures
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model clean') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: configure
commands.short_description: Configure model settings and options
commands.python_alternative: Various itasca.set_* functions

commands.name: cycle
commands.short_description: Execute a specified number of calculation cycles
commands.python_alternative: itasca.cycle(n)

commands.name: deterministic
commands.short_description: Enable/disable deterministic mode for parallel computations
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model deterministic ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: display
commands.short_description: Control display window updates during cycling
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model display ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: domain
commands.short_description: Define computational domain and boundary conditions
commands.python_alternative: itasca.set_domain_min(), itasca.set_domain_max(), itasca.set_domain_condition()

commands.name: gravity
commands.short_description: Set gravity vector or scalar (units of acceleration)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.set_gravity(vector)

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Track model-level properties over time
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model history ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: large-strain
commands.short_description: Enable/disable large-strain formulation for large deformations
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model large-strain ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: mechanical
commands.short_description: Configure mechanical process parameters (timestep, activity, coupling)
commands.python_alternative: Various itasca functions for specific parameters

commands.name: new
commands.short_description: Clear all model-state information to start new problem
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model new') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: orientation-tracking
commands.short_description: Enable/disable orientation tracking for contact models
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model orientation-tracking ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: random
commands.short_description: Set random number generator seed for reproducibility
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model random ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: range
commands.short_description: Create named range for efficient repeated range queries
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model range ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: restore
commands.short_description: Restore model state from file
commands.python_alternative: Use itasca.command('model restore <filename>')

commands.name: results
commands.short_description: Manage lightweight results files for post-processing and movies
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model results ...') - no direct Python SDK method

commands.name: save
commands.short_description: Save model state to file
commands.python_alternative: Use itasca.command('model save <filename>')

commands.name: solve
commands.short_description: Solve to mechanical equilibrium using convergence criteria
commands.python_alternative: itasca.cycle() with manual ratio checking in loop

commands.name: thermal
commands.short_description: Configure thermal process parameters (requires model configure thermal)
commands.python_alternative: Various itasca thermal functions

commands.name: update-interval
commands.short_description: Set update interval for various model operations
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('model update-interval ...') - no direct Python SDK method

notes: 'model solve' is preferred for reaching equilibrium (not available in Python SDK)
'model cycle' is equivalent to itasca.cycle() in Python
Model state can be saved/restored for checkpointing
Domain settings must be configured before generating objects
Use 'model solve' with ratio criteria for automatic convergence

fragment.description: Fragment tracking and analysis commands for connected component detection in contact networks. Fragments identify groups of connected bodies based on contact connectivity.

commands.name: activate
commands.short_description: Activate automatic fragment computation at specified intervals
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('fragment activate ...') - no direct SDK method

commands.name: clear
commands.short_description: Clear fragment tracking data and registration
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('fragment clear') - no direct SDK method

commands.name: compute
commands.short_description: Compute fragments once based on current contact connectivity
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('fragment compute') - no direct SDK method

commands.name: deactivate
commands.short_description: Deactivate automatic fragment computation
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('fragment deactivate') - no direct SDK method

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List fragment information and statistics
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('fragment list') - no direct SDK method

commands.name: register
commands.short_description: Register contact types for fragment analysis (required initialization)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.command('fragment register ...') - no direct SDK method

typical_workflow: 2. Compute or activate: fragment compute OR fragment activate cycles 100
3. Query fragment IDs: ball.fragment() or clump.fragment() in Python
notes: Fragment registration (fragment register) is required before any computation
By default, only bonded contacts contribute to fragment connectivity (use ignorebond for all contacts)
Fragment ID 0 indicates isolated body (no contacts)
fragment activate enables automatic periodic computation; fragment compute is one-time
After computation, query fragment IDs via ball.fragment() or clump.fragment() in Python

measure.description: Measurement region creation and property analysis commands. Measurement regions calculate porosity, stress, strain, coordination number, and particle size distributions within spherical (3D) or circular (2D) regions.

commands.name: create
commands.short_description: Create a measurement region (disk in 2D, sphere in 3D)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.measure module - consult PFC Python documentation

commands.name: delete
commands.short_description: Delete measurement region(s)
commands.python_alternative: itasca.measure module - consult PFC Python documentation

commands.name: dump
commands.short_description: Export measurement data to table
commands.python_alternative: itasca.measure module - consult PFC Python documentation

commands.name: history
commands.short_description: Track measurement properties over time
commands.python_alternative: itasca.measure module - consult PFC Python documentation

commands.name: list
commands.short_description: List measurement region information and results
commands.python_alternative: itasca.measure module - consult PFC Python documentation

commands.name: modify
commands.short_description: Modify measurement region properties
commands.python_alternative: itasca.measure module - consult PFC Python documentation

typical_workflow: 1. Create measurement region: measure create radius 5.0
notes: Measurement regions are disks (2D) or spheres (3D)
Measurements calculated when 'model clean' is called or when FISH intrinsic queries measurement
Can measure: porosity, coordination number, stress, strain, size distribution
Use 'bins' keyword in create to enable particle size distribution calculation
Tolerance parameter controls accuracy vs. speed trade-off for porosity calculation

ball generate.reason: Python SDK doesn't support packing patterns for batch ball creation
ball generate.python_alternative: itasca.ball.create() - can create individual balls only
ball generate.python_workaround: Use itasca.ball.create() in a loop with manual positioning logic

wall generate.reason: Python SDK doesn't support geometric wall generation (box, cylinder, etc.)
wall generate.python_alternative: Manual wall creation using Python SDK requires vertex-by-vertex construction
wall generate.python_workaround: Calculate vertices manually and use itasca.wall module

clump template.reason: Python SDK does not provide clump template management
clump template.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump template ...') - must use command interface

clump generate.reason: Python SDK cannot generate clumps with templates and packing patterns
clump generate.python_alternative: itasca.command('clump generate ...') - must use command interface

model solve.reason: Python SDK doesn't have automatic equilibrium detection
model solve.python_workaround: Use itasca.cycle() with manual ratio checking in a loop

fragment register.reason: Fragment registration is a global initialization operation
fragment register.python_alternative: itasca.command('fragment register ...') - no direct SDK method

notes: Commands are preferred over Python SDK for batch operations and specialized geometries
Python SDK is preferred for fine-grained control and programmatic logic
Use 'itasca.command()' to execute any PFC command from Python
Commands return no value; use 'result' subcommands to query results
Most commands operate on ranges (groups, spatial regions, all objects)
